findings.description: Curmudgeon applies a single adversarial register calibrated for truth-preservation to all items, producing a 4:1 false-positive ratio on the Phase A design-refresh batch (ISS-1334, 1346, 1365, 1366, 1367, 1368). Only 1 real blocker (1367) and 1 post-integration check (1368); the other 4 were design-standards pedantry that the operator closed as wontfix/noted/accepted-fragility.

suggested_fix.find: see PROP-012 for exact find/replace text
suggested_fix.replace: introduce a two-register review model (Register A — truth-preservation; Register B — design-category) with explicit discriminator on each finding and new `advisory` severity class for non-truth-impact design-standards observations. a11y functional carveout keeps WCAG AA failures at major+.
suggested_fix.rationale: Retroactive calibration in PROP-012 shows 6/6 ISS land on operator-compatible dispositions under the new rubric. Advisory class preserves institutional memory without burning operator triage time.

recommendations.description: Review PROP-012 retroactive calibration. Verify the classification of each of the 6 Phase A ISS matches your disposition. If any divergence, flag before applying (especially for ISS-1334 — I classify it as TRUTH-IMPACT/major even though it was closed by clarifications; confirm this is the intended outcome).

Decide migration sequencing for PROP-012. Recommendation: apply curmudgeon.md + analyst.md changes together, then enter the 72h shadow period, and tie the enforcement decision to the next design-category EXP batch (Phase B if it arrives within 72h, else time-based exit).

Write the decider-curmudgeon.md severity-triage mapping for the new `advisory` class. PROP-012 intentionally defers this — the decider prompt text is volatile and a hard-coded find/replace from this tinker run would likely fail to apply cleanly. Specify: advisory default → close as 'advisory-noted' (no open ISS); advisory with `resurface_on` or `post_integration_visual_check` → open low-priority ISS tagged 'advisory-deferred'.
Close PROP-005 as wontfix (13 days old, repeatedly STILL_BROKEN, no-op rate high). Or accept it will remain STILL_BROKEN indefinitely; either is fine but deciding reduces pipeline noise.
Apply PROP-011 before next Mode 3 tinker run. Single-file edit to tinker-cost-engineering.md, low risk, prevents ~5-10 false-positive stale-complete findings per future Mode 3.
PROP-009r2 verification_pattern should drop the CLEAN_CLONE env-gate. Factually applied, but the gate keeps verification tinker-side returning STILL_BROKEN. Small cleanup.
Next Mode 3 tinker (2026-04-24): audit open-issue direction. 91→110 today (+19, slowing but still net-positive). Escalate if day-4 delta >=+15.

counter_arguments_considered.did_i_pick_the_right_mode: The open operator directive makes this Mode 4 straightforward. Alternative was Mode 3 (cost engineering — daily default when healthy), but the directive is a dated deliverable and the previous run already did Mode 3. Re-running Mode 3 while ignoring an open directive would be the wrong choice.
counter_arguments_considered.should_the_prop_have_been_bigger_or_smal: 34KB feels large but the directive asked for 7 specific deliverables (retroactive calibration, exact prompt text, category mechanism, migration plan, rollback plan, a11y carveout, severity labeling) and each got its own section with counter-arguments. Trimming would sacrifice the counter-argument sections, which are the most valuable part of this PROP. I chose fullness.
counter_arguments_considered.am_i_too_confident_about_the_retroactive: The calibration is n=1 (one design batch), and I'm using the same ISS the operator used to articulate the problem — which is arguably cherry-picking. The 72h shadow-run phase is explicitly designed to guard against this: enforcement only happens after a second batch (Phase B) has been seen side-by-side, so calibration gets a second data point before the rubric goes live.
counter_arguments_considered.am_i_asking_the_operator_to_do_too_much_: PROP-012 flags 5 human-judgment points (calibration review, migration sequencing, decider-triage mapping, exact a11y carveout wording, shadow-run duration). This is at the upper end of what I can ask without splitting into sub-PROPs. The directive itself asked for most of these, so the ask is appropriate; but the operator should know they are on the hook for the decider-triage mapping specifically — I left it deferred because hard-coding it would likely fail.
counter_arguments_considered.what_if_the_operator_overrules_a_retroac: The PROP then requires revision. My retroactive calibration for ISS-1334 in particular may be over-strict — I kept it at major/TRUTH-IMPACT even though operator closed by clarifications. If the operator says 'no, that should have been advisory,' the rubric is slightly different and needs tightening.
